title: EXAMINATION - Case W01876040 (W01876040)
text:
OPERATIONS DEPARTMENT
M123
Ex officio refusal of protection (Article 7, Article 42(2) EUTMR)
Alicante, 19/03/2026
Barker Brettell Sweden AB
Kungsbroplan 3
SE-112 27 Stockholm
SUECIA
Your reference: A0161906 99049624 0000000
International registration No: 1876040
Trade Mark:
Name of the holder: Lactea Therapeutics LLC
4539 Metropolitan Ct
Frederick MD 21704
United States
I. Summary of the facts
On 22/10/2025 the Office issued a Notification of ex officio provisional refusal of protection
pursuant to Article 7(1)(b) and (c) EUTMR because it found that the trade mark applied for
is descriptive and devoid of any distinctive character.
The goods for which the provisional refusal was issued were:
Class 30 Chewing gum; non-medicated chewing gum for promoting dental health.
The reasons are set out in the Notification of ex officio provisional refusal of protection,
which forms an integral part of this decision. It is accessible in the Annex of this decision.
II. Summary of the holder’s arguments
On 22/12/2025 the holder requested an extension of the time limit for submitting
observations until 22/02/2026 and this request was granted. However, the holder failed to
submit observations within the extended time limit
III. Reasons
Avenida de Europa, 4 • E - 03008 • Alicante, Spain
Tel. +34 965139100 • www.euipo.europa.eu

Page 2 of 2
Pursuant to Article 94 EUTMR, it is up to the Office to take a decision based on reasons or
evidence on which the holder has had an opportunity to present its comments.
Having received no observations from the holder, the Office has decided to maintain the
grounds for refusal set out in the Notification of ex officio provisional refusal of protection.
IV. Conclusion
For the abovementioned reasons, and pursuant to Article 7(1)(b) and (c) EUTMR, protection
of the international registration No 1876040 is refused for the European Union.
According to Article 67 EUTMR, you have a right to appeal against this decision. According
to Article 68 EUTMR, notice of appeal must be filed in writing at the Office within two months
of the date of notification of this decision. It must be filed in the language of the proceedings
in which the decision subject to appeal was taken. Furthermore, a written statement of the
grounds of appeal must be filed within four months of the same date. The notice of appeal
will be deemed to be filed only when the appeal fee of EUR 720 has been paid.
Colm Purcell

OPERATIONS DEPARTMENT
W110
Notification of ex officio provisional total refusal of protection
(Article 5 of the Madrid Protocol, Rule 17(1) and (2) of the Regulations under
the Protocol relating to the Madrid Agreement, and Article 33 EUTMIR)
Alicante, 22/10/2025
BEGINNING OF TIME LIMIT: 22/10/2025
END OF TIME LIMIT: 22/12/2025
International registration number: 1876040
Trade mark:
Name of the holder: Lactea Therapeutics LLC
Protection of the abovementioned mark is provisionally refused for the European Union for
all the goods and services covered by the designation of the European Union.
I. Grounds
The Office has examined your international registration designating the European Union to
ensure that it does not fall under any of the grounds for refusal established under Article 7
EUTMR.
The sign
The application consists of the shape mark ‘ ’.
Legal basis for the objection
Article 7(1)(b) EUTMR
Avenida de Europa, 4 • E - 03008 • Alicante, Spain
Tel. +34 965139100 • www.euipo.europa.eu

Page 2 of 3
The shape mark you have applied for is ineligible for registration under Article 7(1)(b)
EUTMR because it is devoid of any distinctive character for the goods for which protection is
sought.
Lack of distinctiveness
The goods for which this objection is raised are:
Class 30 Chewing gum; Non-medicated chewing gum for promoting dental health.
The sign consists merely of a simple 3D pentagon shape. Basic geometric shapes are, by
their nature, devoid of inherent distinctiveness.
The relevant consumer would perceive them as a simple geometric form, prima facie
incapable of transmitting any trade mark message. Basic geometrical shapes (such as
rectangles, rhombus and other quadrilaterals) are unable to convey, per se, any message
that can be remembered by consumers and will accordingly not be seen by them as an
indication of the commercial origin (12/09/2007, T-304/05, Pentagon, EU:T:2012:271, § 22).
Such elements are essentially decorative and, for this reason, are also non-distinctive.
The sign does not contain any elements that may be easily and instantly memorized by the
relevant public. It will be perceived only as a decorative element or a functional variant of a
common product shape. Taken as a whole, the mark applied for will be perceived as an
excessively simple geometric shape.
Therefore, the sign is devoid of any distinctive character within the meaning of Article 7(1)(b)
EUTMR.
II. Representation
The holder of the international registration is obliged to be represented before the Office by a
legal practitioner or professional representative that is entitled to represent third parties
before the EUIPO (Articles 119(2) and 120(1) EUTMR). Protection of the international
registration for the European Union will be refused in whole if a representative is not
appointed within the time limit indicated below.
III. Time Limit
The holder of the international registration is hereby given a time limit of two months to
overcome the grounds for refusal indicated under point I above and to comply with the
requirements indicated under point II above. This time limit will start to run on the day the
Office issues this notification (Article 193(2), (3) and (4) EUTMR). Any reply to this
communication must be addressed to the EUIPO only.
If you do not send any reply within the time limit, the Office will issue an appealable decision
refusing the protection of the international registration for the European Union.
If you need additional explanation on any aspect of this communication, please contact the
EUIPO’s Information Centre on +34 965139100 making reference to your application
number. The Information Centre will provide an answer to your question, or will put you
through to the examiner in charge of the file. If the examiner is not available, you may
request a ‘call back’ and the examiner will contact you within two working days.

Page 3 of 3
Colm Purcell
Examiner

in accordance with the Office's initiative for quality enhancing and sharing knowledge,
introduced by Decision No EX-20-06 of the Executive Director.
